title: Mainboard assembly with antenna and capable of realizing sub-6G and communication equipment
abstract: The invention discloses a mainboard assembly with an antenna and capable of realizing sub-6G and communication equipment, the mainboard assembly comprises a mainboard and a patch antenna arranged on the mainboard, and the mainboard comprises a substrate and a mainboard ground extension structure connected to one end of the substrate; the substrate comprises a clearance area opposite to the mainboard ground extension structure, and the patch antenna is attached to the interior of the clearance area; the mainboard ground extension structure is coupled with the clearance area and the patch antenna to realize a sub-6G frequency band; according to the mainboard assembly, the mainboard ground extension structure connected with the substrate is arranged; the mainboard ground extension structure,the clearance area and the patch antenna are coupled; the requirements of N41, N77, N78 and N79 frequency band transmission are met, so the Sub-6G requirement with high-speed signal transmission is met

title: FACILITATING DYNAMIC MULTIPLE PUBLIC LAND MOBILE NETWORK RESOURCE MANAGEMENT IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating dynamic satellite and mobility convergence for mobility backhaul in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise dividing resources of a wireless network between a first network device and a second network device based on defined service level agreements. The operations also can comprise receiving a data packet from a mobile device. The data packet can comprise an indication that the first network device provides services for the mobile device. Further, the operations can comprise transferring the data packet to the first network device based on the resources assigned to the first network device and based on the data packet bypassing an access core of the wireless network.

title: FACILITATING DYNAMIC SATELLITE AND MOBILITY CONVERGENCE FOR MOBILITY BACKHAUL IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating dynamic satellite and mobility convergence for mobility backhaul in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise determining that a group of user equipment devices are located in a defined geographic area and are consuming more than a defined level of resources of a wireless communications network based on an amount of network traffic received from the group of user equipment devices. The operations also can comprise configuring an integrated network comprising a first group of terrestrial network devices and a second group of satellite network devices. Further, the operations can comprise routing at least a portion of network traffic associated with the group of user equipment devices among the first group of terrestrial network devices and the second group of satellite network devices.

title: Coating forming method of novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a coating forming method of a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board. The coating forming method comprises the following steps: (1) coating a copper foil to synthesize a liquid film; (2) sending the copper foil into an oven for baking, and forming a cured film on the copper foil to obtain a single-sided board; (3) coating a synthetic liquid high-frequency material layer on the cured film; and (4) sending to a tunnel oven for baking, synthesizing a liquid high-frequency material layer into a semi-cured high-frequency material layer, and obtaining the novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board. The invention further discloses a product prepared by implementing the method. The prepared novel material layer structure ofthe high-frequency circuit board has the performance of transmitting high-frequency signals at a high speed, can adapt to the high-frequency and high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal app

title: Preparation method of novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a preparation method of a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board. The preparation method comprises the following steps: (1) coating a cured PI film tosynthesize a liquid TFP film; (2) feeding into a tunnel oven for segmented baking, and forming a semi-cured TFP film on the front surface of the cured PI film; and (3) hot-pressing a copper foil on the semi-cured TFP film to obtain the single-sided novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board. The invention further discloses a novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board, which is prepared by implementing the method. The prepared novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board has the performance of transmitting high-frequency signals at a high speed, can adapt to the high-frequency and high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal application at present, and is particularly suitable for novel 5G and 6G scientific and te

title: Manufacturing method of multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a manufacturing method of a multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board. The manufacturing method comprises the following steps: (1) manufacturing a double-sided FPC flexible board; (2) manufacturing at least one group of soft material layer structures; (3) manufacturing at least two groups of hard material layer structures; and (4) hot-pressing at least one group of soft material layer structures on the circuit on the upper surface and/or the lower surface of the double-sided FPC flexible board, and respectively hot-pressing at least one group of hard material layer structures on the outermost circuit above and below the base film of the double-sided FPC flexible board to obtain the multilayer double-sided flexible circuit board. The invention also discloses a multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board. The manufacturing process is simplified and convenient; the manufactured multilayer double-sided rigid-flex board has high-frequency characteristics, namely has thep

title: Press-fit forming method of novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a press-fit forming method of a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board, which comprises the following steps of: (1) coating a semi-cured film on a film to form a TPI film; (2) putting a copper foil on the TPI film for hot pressing, curing the semi-cured film, and integrating the semi-cured film with the film to form a synthetic film; and (3) coating the other surface of the film with a layer of semi-cured high-frequency material to form the novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board. The invention further discloses a novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board, which is prepared by implementing the method. The manufactured novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board has the performance of transmitting high-frequency signals at a high speed, can adapt to the high-frequency and high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal application at present, and is particula

title: Facilitation of dynamic edge computations for 6G or other next generation network
abstract: Peripheral devices such as wearables can link to mobile devices to access network elements. These peripheral devices can comprise a communication part and a processing part. Each of these parts, specially the processing part of the next item in a communication chain, can be multiple times larger and stronger than the previous linked item. For instance, the peripheral device can be connected to the mobile device which can be in line connected to an NodeB device. Because the processing power of mobile device can be several times stronger than the peripheral, a mobile edge computing platform and a multi-access application function facilitate sharing of processing power capabilities between the peripheral devices, mobile devices, and/or the NodeB devices.

title: Capacity expansion method for 6G communication cellular mobile communication system
assignee: SUZHOU YUNZHIQIU INTELLIGENT TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a capacity expansion method for a 6G communication cellular mobile communication system. Cellular mobile communication adopts a cellular wireless networking mode, a terminal and network equipment are connected through a wireless channel so as to realize mutual communication of users in activities, and the capacity expansion method is characterized by comprising the following five steps. The method comprises the following steps that: 1, macro cells are adopted, and more macro cells with reduced coverage area are constructed by adopting a cell splitting technology; 2, a densification frequency reuse scheme or an MrP multi-reuse technology is adopted, isolated microcells and picocells are established in hot spot areas and important places, and sometimes, the first stepand the second step can be carried out in a crossed and combined mode; and 3, the capacity of the 6G communication cellular mobile communication system is expanded by improving the voice coding rate,r

title: Antenna structure and wearable electronic device having antenna structure
assignee: YULONG COMPUTER TELECOMMUNICATION SCIENTIFIC SHENZHEN CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides an antenna structure, which is applied to a wearable electronic device. The wearable electronic device includes a display module. The antenna structure is arranged on the display module. The antenna structure includes a carrier and multiple groups of array antennas. The carrier is arranged along the periphery of the display module. The multiple groups of array antennas, spaced apart from each other along the carrier, are used for receiving and transmitting sub 6G millimeter waves. The invention also provides an electronic device having the antenna structure.

title: RECONSTITUTED SUBSTRATE FOR RADIO FREQUENCY APPLICATIONS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods and apparatus for forming thin-form-factor reconstituted substrates and semiconductor device packages for radio frequency applications. The substrate and package structures described herein may be utilized in high-density 2D and 3D integrated devices for 4G, 5G, 6G, and other wireless network systems. In one embodiment, a silicon substrate is structured by laser ablation to include cavities for placement of semiconductor dies and vias for deposition of conductive interconnections. Additionally, one or more cavities are structured to be filled or occupied with a flowable dielectric material. Integration of one or more radio frequency components adjacent the dielectric-filled cavities enables improved performance of the radio frequency elements with reduced signal loss caused by the silicon substrate.

title: CELLULAR SYSTEM
abstract: A system includes a housing with one or more edge processors to handle processing on behalf of a mobile target or to provide local data to the mobile target or to provide artificial intelligence for the mobile target; one or more antennas coupled to the housing; and a processor to control a directionality of the antennas in communication with the mobile target using 5G or 6G protocols.

title: CELLULAR SYSTEM
abstract: A system includes a cellular transceiver to communicate with a predetermined target; one or more antennas coupled to the 5G or 6G transceiver each electrically or mechanically steerable to the predetermined target; a processor to control a directionality of the one or more antennas in communication with the predetermined target; and an edge processing module coupled to the processor and the one or more antennas to provide low-latency computation for the predetermined target.

title: Three-dimensional hairpin filter applied to 6G communication
abstract: The invention discloses a three-dimensional hairpin filter applied to 6G communication. The hairpin filter comprises a hairpin unit column and an upper grounding RDL which are arranged in parallel, the hairpin unit column is formed by arranging a plurality of hairpin units at intervals; a signal TSV is arranged at one end of the hairpin unit; the other opposite end is a signal RDL; the upper and lower end surface structures of the adjacent hairpin units are opposite; symmetrical signal feeders are arranged at the tops of the two ends of the hairpin unit column. Signal RDLs are arranged on thetop surfaces of the hairpin units at the end parts of the two ends of the hairpin unit column; the top of the upper grounding RDL is provided with a grounding feeder line opposite to the signal feederline, the bottom of the upper grounding RDL is provided with a lower grounding RDL, a grounding TSV opposite to the signal TSV is arranged between the upper grounding RDL and the lower grounding RDL,an

title: Method of detecting rod inclination by receiving signal strength data of rod
assignee: SHANGHAI LUHUI INTELLIGENT SYSTEM CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a method for detecting the rod inclination by receiving signal strength data of the rod, which comprises the following steps: defining data content and meaning of a 4G/5G/6G micro base station signal receiving end, such as time scale, signal strength, ID of a sector cell and communication capability; defining an antenna of a base station signal transmitting end; ensuring the stability of base station signals; carrying out big data analysis under normal conditions, and eliminating instability of base station signals by using big data; on the basis that the base stationsignal is approximately stable, analyzing the orientation change and inclination angle change degree caused by rod inclination and the influence on the signal intensity of a large-range receiving end;upgrading algorithms. The inclination of the rod can be directly detected through the signal intensity; data analysis can be completed in a large-scale range based on signals provided by a three-directi

title: Manufacturing method of multilayer flexible circuit board and product thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a manufacturing method of a multilayer flexible circuit board. The manufacturing method comprises the following steps: (1) manufacturing a double-sided FPC flexible board; (2)manufacturing a novel material layer structure; (3) hot-pressing at least one group of novel material layer structures on the circuit on the upper surface and/or the lower surface of the double-sidedFPC flexible board; and forming a protective layer on the circuit of the outermost novel material layer structure and/or the exposed circuit of the double-sided FPC flexible board to obtain the multi-layer flexible circuit board. The invention further discloses the multi-layer flexible circuit board manufactured by implementing the method. The manufacturing process is simplified and convenient, and the production efficiency is high; the manufactured multi-layer flexible circuit board not only greatly simplifies the structure of a novel material layer and reduces the overall thickness, but alsoha

title: FACILITATING OUTER LOOP LINK ADAPTATION IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating facilitate enablement of a smart HARQ feedback to support outer loop link adaptation in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise sending, by a first device comprising a processor, a data packet to a second device. The method also can comprise receiving, by the first device, negative acknowledgement data from the second device. The negative acknowledgement data can indicate that the second device fails to support a modulation and coding protocol of the data packet.

title: FACILITATING A GROUP HYBRID AUTOMATIC REPEAT REQUEST PROCEDURE FOR SIDELINK GROUP CASE IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating a group hybrid automatic repeat request procedure for sidelink group-case in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining, by a first device comprising a processor, that first information received via a sidelink control channel is successfully decoded and that second information received via a sidelink shared channel is not successfully decoded. The first information and the second information can be received from a second device. Further, the method can comprise transmitting, by the first device, negative acknowledgement data based on a group hybrid automatic repeat request feedback procedure. The method also can comprise receiving, by the first device, retransmission of the second information via the sidelink shared channel from a group of devices in response to the negative acknowledgement data.

title: Symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6G communication application frequency band antenna
abstract: The invention discloses a symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6G communication application frequency band antenna, and belongs to the field of terahertz antennas. The symmetrical multi-slot terahertz 6Gcommunication application frequency band antenna is characterized in that the main body part of the antenna is an insulating dielectric layer; a butterfly-like radiation patch and a circular patch combined with the butterfly-like radiation patch are arranged in the center of the upper surface of the dielectric layer; the butterfly-like radiation patch and the circular patch are used for feeding together; the circular patch is combined in the radiation patch, so that ultra-wideband effect is achieved; and semicircular grooves are symmetrically formed in the left end and the right end of the radiation patch towards the farthest end of the center of the radiation patch, two sets of four rectangular grooves with the same width are formed in the left end and the right end of the radiation patcht

title: FACILITATING SPARSITY ADAPTIVE FEEDBACK IN THE DELAY DOPPLER DOMAIN IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating sparsity adaptive feedback in the delay doppler domain in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining, by a first device comprising a processor, a channel covariance matrix in a time-frequency domain based on a channel estimation associated with reference signals received from a second device. The method also can comprise decomposing, by the first device, the channel covariance matrix into a group of component matrices. Further, the method can comprise transforming, by the first device, respective matrices of the group of component matrices into respective covariance matrices in a delay doppler domain. The method also can comprise determining, by the first device, channel state information feedback in the delay doppler domain.

title: Random laser
abstract: The utility model discloses a random laser. The fiber laser comprises a pumping source and a fiber network formed by randomly distributing a plurality of optical fibers, the optical fiber comprises acore layer and a cladding layer, the core layer is a PMMA layer (polymethyl methacrylate layer) doped with rhodamine 6G, the cladding layer is a PVDF layer (polyvinylidene fluoride layer), the refractive index of the cladding layer is larger than that of air, and the refractive index of the core layer is larger than that of the cladding layer. Wherein the pumping source carries out pumping in an oblique incidence mode, light is effectively coupled at the intersection point of optical fibers in the fiber network through evanescent waves to form a random annular resonant cavity, and stimulated radiation light is subjected to selective gain amplification in the random annular resonant cavity to form laser. According to the utility model, while directional output of random laser is ensured, the

title: Cylindrical lithium ion battery with quick charging performance
assignee: TIANJIN SPACE POWER TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a cylindrical lithium ion battery with quick charging performance, and belongs to the technical field of lithium ion batteries. The cylindrical lithium ion battery comprises apositive pole piece, a negative pole piece, electrolyte, a diaphragm and a shell, and is characterized in that the positive pole piece comprises a positive pole material and a current collector, and the positive pole material is composed of an active substance, a conductive agent and a binder; the coating density of the positive pole piece is 15-35mg/cm<2>, and the compaction density of the positive pole piece is 2.8-3.6g/cm<3>; the negative pole piece comprises a negative pole material and a current collector, and the negative pole material is composed of an active substance, a conductive agent and a binder; the coating density of the negative pole piece is 9-22mg/cm<2>, and the compaction density of the negative pole piece is 1.4-1.70g/cm<3>; the diaphragm is one of a PE film, a PP/PE/PPc

title: Macro station user distribution method for unmanned aerial vehicle auxiliary cellular network
abstract: In order to solve the problems that a B5G/6G network faces higher coverage and larger capacity requirements and an existing ground cellular network is difficult to guarantee the performance of edge users, the embodiment of the invention provides a macro station user distribution scheme for an unmanned aerial vehicle auxiliary cellular network, so that dynamic coverage is provided for the edge users. The method is beneficial to high channel gain brought by a large-probability line-of-sight link of an unmanned aerial vehicle base station; according to the scheme, one or more unmanned aerial vehicles take the ground macro base station as the center and do circular motion in the clockwise or anticlockwise direction, and a high data transmission rate is provided for macro cell users by utilizing the sight distance link and the antenna gain. By adjusting parameters such as the movement track radius, the flight height, the flight speed and the transmitting power of the unmanned aerial vehicle

title: Sub-6G multi-band antenna system and mobile terminal
assignee: KUNSHAN YIQU INFORMATION TECH RESEARCH INSTITUTE CO LTD
abstract: The utility model provides a Sub6G multi-band antenna system and a mobile terminal, the antenna system comprises a metal floor, a first radiation arm, a second radiation arm and a matching tuning network, the first radiation arm comprises a first metal segment and a second metal segment, one end of the first metal segment is connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the second metal segment is connected to the matching tuning network. The other end of the first metal section is connected to the second metal section, the second radiation arm comprises a third metal section and a fourthmetal section, one end of the third metal section is connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the third metal section is connected to the fourth metal section; the length of the first radiation arm is 1030 mm, the length of the second radiation arm is 410 mm, and the distance between the second metal section and the metal floor and the distance between the fourth metal section and the me

title: Sub-6G multi-band antenna system and mobile terminal
assignee: KUNSHAN YIQU INFORMATION TECH RESEARCH INSTITUTE CO LTD
abstract: The utility model provides a Sub6G multi-band antenna system and a mobile terminal, the antenna system comprises a metal floor, a first radiation arm and a second radiation arm, the first radiation arm and the second radiation arm are arranged on the same side of the metal floor, the first radiation arm comprises a first metal segment and a second metal segment, one end of the first metal segmentis connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the second metal segment is connected to the mobile terminal. The other end of the first metal section is connected to the second metal section, the second radiation arm comprises a third metal section and a fourth metal section, one end of the third metal section is connected to the metal floor, and the other end of the third metal section isconnected to the fourth metal section; the length of the first radiation arm is 1030 mm, the length of the second radiation arm is 410 mm, and the distance between the second metal section and the metal

title: Method for reducing task unloading delay of 6G digital twin edge computing network
abstract: The invention discloses a method for reducing task unloading delay of a 6G digital twin edge computing network. The method mainly provides a new digital twin edge computing network, uses digital twinto estimate the state of an edge server so as to provide training data, formalizes a series of unloading decision optimization problems in a user moving process, uses a Lyapunov optimization method tosimplify a long-term migration cost constraint into a multi-target dynamic optimization problem, and finally, a deep learning framework based on Actor-Critic is used for solving the calculation unloading optimization problem, and the training Agent is realized by a digital twin edge calculation network. According to the method, the unloading delay, the task failure rate and the migration rate arereduced to the maximum extent on the premise of keeping relatively low system cost.

title: Two-stage temperature adjusting system based on critical temperature of organic heat carrier
abstract: The invention provides a two-stage temperature regulating system based on the critical temperature of an organic heat carrier, which can provide continuous and stable heating/cooling/constant-temperature regulation for a large-scale, multilayer or multiple production lines at the critical temperature of the organic heat carrier of 410 DEG C, and the regulation error during continuous heat supply or cooling is less than 0.5 DEG C; and the energy consumption is reduced by 30-40% by adopting a two-stage temperature regulation process flow. While energy consumption is greatly reduced, through high-temperature stable convection heat transfer, due to the fact that the process curve is smooth and the temperature gradient is small, the heat uniformity of a large-specification heat plate of a large-scale multilayer hot press or a plurality of hot presses is kept within 0.5-1.0 DEG C. The two-stage adjusting system provided by the invention can meet the production and processing requirements ofl

title: 5G antenna and electronic equipment
assignee: SHENZHEN REALME MOBILE COMMUNICATION CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a 5G antenna and electronic equipment. The 5G antenna comprises a monopole and a parasitic unit. The monopole comprises a main body and an elastic piece, the main body comprises a first end and a second end which are opposite, the distance between the elastic piece and the first end is greater than that between the elastic piece and the second end, the elastic piece is usedfor connecting an excitation source, the parasitic unit is arranged opposite to the second end, the parasitic unit comprises a first branch knot and a second branch knot, the first branch knot is provided with a first groove, at least part of the main body and at least part of the elastic piece extend into the first groove, and the distance between the second branch knot and the second end is greater than that between the first branch knot and the second end, and the connecting piece is used for connecting the first branch and the second branch. According to the 5G antenna, the electronic equi

title: Structure multiplexing-based miniaturized high-isolation common-caliber antenna
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of common-caliber antennas and particularly provides a structure multiplexing-based miniaturized high-isolation common-caliber antenna used for further reducing the occupied area of the common-caliber antenna. According to the structure multiplexing-based miniaturized high-isolation common-caliber antenna, firstly, a short-circuit point is introduced into the center of a patch antenna by using the characteristic that an electric field of the patch antenna is zero at the center of the patch antenna, so that the occupied area of the patch antenna is reduced on the basis of not changing the operating frequency of the patch antenna, thereby achieving the target of miniaturizing a sub-6G patch antenna; secondly, a substrate integrated waveguide antenna is multiplexed with a feed structure as the patch antenna, thereby achieving the common-caliber design of the substrate integrated waveguide antenna and the patch antenna; and finally the miniaturi

title: FPGA-BASED RATE-ADAPTIVE SPATIALLY-COUPLED LDPC CODES FOR OPTICAL COMMUNICATIONS
abstract: Disclosed are systems, methods, and software for generating spatially-coupled low-density parity-check (SC-LDPC) codes. A method for generating SC-LDPC codes includes generating one or more quasi-cyclic low-density parity-check (QC-LDPC) codes, and also includes assigning at least one of the generated one or more QC-LDPC codes as one or more template codes. The method further includes copying at least a portion of the one or more template codes to introduce irregularity. The method also includes shifting one or more template codes on a sub-block basis to generate at least one SC-LDPC code. As compared to known LDPC code generation modalities, the disclosed invention provides a simplified technique for implementation in streamlined hardware which has more general applicability across both present, and anticipated, communication systems, including those adapted for use with optical communications, wireless communications, and 5G as well as future 6G.

title: ANTENNA ASSEMBLY FOR TERMINAL WITH FOLDABLE SCREEN AND TERMINAL
abstract: A terminal with a foldable screen and an antenna assembly thereof are disclosed. The antenna assembly includes a foldable frame and seven antenna modules. The frame includes a first side frame; a second side frame opposite to the first side frame, a third side frame, and a fourth side frame. A first antenna portion is disposed at a corner connecting the first side frame to the third side frame. A second antenna portion is disposed at a corner connecting the second side frame and the third side frame. A third antenna portion is disposed on the second sub-frame. Fourth, fifth, and sixth antenna portions are sequentially arranged on the fourth side frame. A seventh antenna portion is disposed on the fourth sub-frame. At least 2*2 MIMO configuration of WIFI frequency band and 4*4 MIMO configuration of 5G NR frequency band below Sub-6G frequency band are formed by the antenna modules.

title: Device for detecting stability of Sub 6G frequency band of wireless 5G product
abstract: The utility model discloses a wireless 5G product Sub 6G frequency band stability detection device which comprises a mounting plate, through holes are formed in the front end face and the rear end face of the mounting plate, connecting blocks are arranged in the through holes and extend out of the mounting plate, and fixing grooves are formed in the peripheries of the outer surfaces of the connecting blocks. A connecting piece is fixedly mounted on the front end face of the connecting block, a detection device body is fixedly mounted on the front end face of the connecting piece, threaded holes are formed in the two sides of the outer surface of the mounting plate correspondingly, moving grooves are formed in the opposite sides of the two threaded holes correspondingly, and movable groovesare formed in the inner sides of the two moving grooves correspondingly; and a screw rod is arranged on the inner side of the moving groove. According to the utility model, no tools are needed when th

title: Broadband band-pass filter for sixth-generation mobile communication and resonance module of broadband band-pass filter
abstract: The invention relates to a broadband band-pass filter for sixth-generation mobile communication and a resonance module of the broadband band-pass filter. The resonance module comprises four layers offrequency selection surface electromagnetic resonance units which are arranged at equal intervals, each frequency selection surface electromagnetic resonance unit comprises a square block layer, wherein the square block layer is provided with three square frames which are sequentially nested from inside to outside by taking the center of the square block layer as the center, the three square frames are all formed by hollowing out, and every two adjacent square frames are connected at an angle of 45 degrees. Compared with the prior art, the filter is oriented to sixth-generation mobile communication, realizes 0.7THz bandwidth, and has 90% high transmittance.

title: 6G terahertz satellite communication receiving device
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G terahertz satellite communication receiving device, which comprises a mounting base and a rotary connecting base. A sliding plate is fixedly connected to the upper end ofthe rotary connecting base, a supporting device is slidably connected into the sliding plate, an adjusting device is fixedly connected to the rear end of the sliding plate, a rotating device is fixedly mounted on the left portion of the supporting device, a communication receiving body is fixedly connected to the middle of the rotating device, a first connecting rod is fixedly connected to the front side of the lower portion of the communication receiving body, a signal receiving structure is fixedly connected to the end, away from the communication receiving body, of the first connecting rod,a first motor is fixedly connected into the mounting base, the output end of the first motor is fixedly connected with a driving gear, and the lower end of the rotary connecting base is fixedly connect

title: Implementation method of compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device
abstract: The invention discloses an implementation method of a compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device, the compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device implemented by adopting the method has three metal layers, and two dielectric plates are arranged among the three metal layers; the top metal layer is an antenna array layer (100); the middle metal layer is a metal ground layer (110), and a plurality of slotting gaps (51, 52, 53 and 5n) arranged according to a certain set position are formed in one side of the metal ground layer (110); the bottom metal layer (120) is an electromagnetic lens (130); the compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device realized by adopting the method can form radiation beams in different directions, realizes electronic scanning,and can be used for scenes of 5G and 6G millimeter wave communication, millimeter wave satellite communication, millimeter wave radar and the like; and according to the technical scheme, compared with

title: 6G communication antenna array element position tolerance determination method based on channel capacity sensitivity
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G communication antenna array element position tolerance determination method based on channel capacity sensitivity, and the method comprises the steps: determining that a design index of the array element position tolerance is the electric field intensity of an array antenna, and calculating the electrical performance of the array antenna under an ideal condition; calculating the array element position error sensitivity and describing the increasing speed of the array element tolerance in iteration; generating an array element random normal error sample; calculatingantenna electrical performance change conditions corresponding to all the samples; judging the number of error samples meeting performance requirements; calculating the tolerance of the last iteration and outputting the array element position tolerance for the electric field intensity and the channel capacity of the array antenna; and comparing the position tolerances, taking the smaller positiont

title: ANTENNA MODULE FOR USE IN FOLDING SCREEN TERMINAL AND TERMINAL
assignee: AAC ACOUSTIC TECH SHENZHEN CO LTD [CN]
abstract: Provided in the present application are an antenna module for use in a folding screen terminal and a terminal comprising said antenna module, the antenna module comprising a folding frame and seven antenna modules; the frame comprises a first edge frame comprising fifth and sixth edge frames arranged symmetrically and a second side frame comprising seventh and eighth edge frames arranged symmetrically arranged opposite to one another, and third and fourth side edge frames arranged opposite one another; a first antenna part is arranged at the connecting point of the first edge frame and the third side edge frame; a second antenna part is arranged at the connecting point of the second edge frame and the third side edge frame; a third antenna part is arranged on the sixth edge frame; fourth, fifth, and sixth antenna parts are arranged at intervals on the fourth side edge frame; and a seventh antenna part is arranged on the eighth edge frame; the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth,

title: Horn antenna and electronic equipment
assignee: HEFEI LCFC INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a horn antenna and electronic equipment. The horn antenna comprises a horn body, a first antenna, a plurality of frequency modulation circuits and a switch, the first antenna,the frequency modulation circuits and the switch are arranged on the inner surface of the horn body. Each frequency modulation circuit is electrically connected with the first antenna through the switch; and the switch is further electrically connected with a controller, and the switch is used for receiving a control signal of the controller and conducting a corresponding frequency modulation circuit according to the control signal so as to adjust the working frequency of the first antenna. According to the invention, Sub-6G, MIMO and WLAN antennas are integrated on the horn body, and a change-over switch and a plurality of frequency modulation circuits are added, so that the antenna can be adaptively adjusted at a low frequency, more internal space of a machine is saved, the design of a na

title: User-centric unmanned aerial vehicle base station multi-beam joint transmission method
abstract: An unmanned aerial vehicle base station becomes an important candidate solution for future B5G/6G network coverage and capacity enhancement by means of high mobility, higher line-of-sight probabilityand flexible deployment characteristics. However, the communication capability of a single unmanned aerial vehicle is limited, and durable and stable services cannot be provided for users. Meanwhile,the large-probability line-of-sight link of the unmanned aerial vehicle also brings strong same-frequency interference while improving the quality of a service link. Therefore, in order to ensure theconsistency and continuity of unmanned aerial vehicle network services, the invention provides an unmanned aerial vehicle base station group taking a user as a center, and unmanned aerial vehicles inthe group serve the user at the same time in a joint transmission (JT) mode so as to enhance the robustness of data transmission. Particularly, each unmanned aerial vehicle is provided with F antennasto p

title: Spectrally encapsulated orthogonal frequency division multiplexing method
abstract: The out-of-band emission (OoBE) problem of cyclic prefix (CP) orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM), which has been unresolved in 4G techniques, needs to be resolved for efficient frequency resource utilization in 6G mobile communication techniques. This research is for specific implementation of orthogonalization, which is a core technique for spectral encapsulation (SE) resulting in OFDM signal spectrum formation. SE-OFDM uses no long filters, is a spectral shaping technique based on zero insertion, and thus is applicable to a short OFDM symbol. According to the present invention, a precoder is generated with an accurate orthogonal matrix and complete blocking is performed at the frequency bandwidth boundary. The information transmission part of SE-OFDM of the present invention is identical to CP-OFDM. Accordingly, it can be realized using IFFT with the BER performance of CP-OFDM retained. As a result, substantial improvement can be achieved in terms of complexity.

title: Copper alloy material for 6G communication connector and preparation method of copper alloy material
assignee: GRINM ENG TECH RES INST CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a copper alloy material for a 6G communication connector and a preparation method of the copper alloy material, and belongs to the technical field of non-ferrous metal processing. The copper alloy material is composed of 3.4-5.5 wt% of Ni, 0.6-1.5 wt% of Si, 0.6-1.2 wt% of Ti, 0.1-0.3 wt% of Cr, 0.01-1.0 wt% of other elements and the balance Cu. According to the copper alloy material for the 6G communication connector and the preparation method of the copper alloy material, the tensile strength of the copper alloy material is 950-1100 MPa, the elongation is 5-15%, the conductivity is 18-35% IACS, the stress relaxation rate of 150 DEG C*1000 h is 10-20%, no defect occurs when the copper alloy material is bent by 90 degrees in the transverse direction and the longitudinal direction when R/T is equal to 3, and the copper alloy material has good comprehensive performance and can meet the use requirement of the 6G communication connector.

title: Intelligent LED garden lamp
assignee: DONGGUAN PERFECT SCIENCE & TECH CO LTD
abstract: The utility model discloses an intelligent LED garden lamp which comprises a lamp post, a main lamp wall, an auxiliary lamp wall, an LED lamp holder, an LED module, an LED lantern, a centralized controller, a storage battery box, a 5G/6G base station antenna and a power generation device, a light sensor is arranged on the centralized controller, a storage battery is arranged in the storage batterybox, and single lamp controllers are arranged in the LED lamp holder, the LED module and the LED lantern. The integrated controller is electrically connected with the three single-lamp controllers, the storage battery, the 5G/6G base station antenna and the power generation device, and the storage battery is electrically connected with the power generation device. The LED street lamp has the advantages that the three types of LED light source components are suitable for lighting lanes, sidewalks and festivals and holiday celebrations; the illumination state of the LED light source part can bea

title: Back-drilling composite cover plate for controlling drilling depth of circuit board and processing method
abstract: The invention relates to a back-drilling composite cover plate for controlling the drilling depth of a circuit board and a processing method. Along with the popularization of 4G, 5G and future 6G communication, a back-drilling process becomes an indispensable manufacturing process in the manufacturing process of the circuit board for communication, and the back-drilling composite cover plate comprises a processing surface insulating layer, a conductive layer carrier and a conductive layer; the conductive layer carrier and the conductive layer are combined in an electroplating, vacuum evaporation deposition or coating manner; the processing surface insulating layer and the conductive layer carrier are combined in a bonding, fitting or pressing manner; due to the adoption of the composite structure, on the premise of ensuring the drilling precision, the selection range of the insulating layer material of the processing surface can be wider, for example, an insulating environment-friendly

title: MACHINE LEARNING BASED NETWORK INTELLIGENTIZATION FOR AUTOMATICALLY- CONFIGURABLE CELLULAR COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: MACHINE LEARNING BASED NETWORK INTELLIGENTIZATION FOR AUTOMATICALLY- CONFIGURABLE CELLULAR COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS With the growing growth of intelligent interfaces and communications infrastructure, as well as diversification technologies with vibrant criteria, existing networks will not be sufficient to entirely satisfy progressively increasing traffic criteria. As a result, analysis on 6 G networks has also been conducted by all industries and education. Artificial Intelligence has recently been used as a modem model for the architecture and automation of 6 G networks with a high degree of intelligence. A-enabled smart infrastructure for 6 G networks for information discovery, intelligent resource management, automated network adaptation and automated service delivery, where infrastructure is split into four levels: wearable devices layer, data mining and analytics layer, remote communication module and smart device layer. title: Film material of bridge island type conductive substance
abstract: The invention discovers and proposes for the first time that the conductive layer of the conductive substance film material has the phenomena of ohmic conduction and capacitive conduction, and also has the phenomenon of inductive conduction (inductive reactance). The film material of the bridge island type conductive material is a conductive film for overcoming inductive reactance on the surface of the conductive film, and bridge island type physical contact (sintering) points are constructed for the conductive materials with different shapes, so that the control on the micro current rheological direction is realized, the high-frequency alternating inductive reactance (or reverse utilization) of a conductive ink layer is reduced, and the film material is used for each product process and has outstanding cost performance; and hot melt pressure bonding, electronic printing, compounding, light, heat, electronic curing, and etching may be performed. title: Pillar-shaped semiconductor device and manufacturing method thereof
abstract: In a SRAM cell, a Si pillar 6a, which is a selection SGT in Si pillars 6a to 6e in an upper row, is located on the left-end in X direction. A Si pillar 6j, which is a selection SGT in Si pillars 6f to 6j in a lower row, is located on the right end. The Si pillar 6g of the lower row is present in a width of an area extended from a contact hole 47a in Y direction in planar view. Then, the Si pillar 6d of the upper row is present in a width of an area extended from a contact hole 47b in Y direction in planar view. In the upper row, a TiN layer 40b, which is a gate electrode for a loading SGT and a driving SGT, is formed to contact at side surface of entire gate region in a vertical direction between the Si pillars 6c, 6d, and 6e. Similarly, in the lower row, a TiN layer 40c, which is a gate electrode for the loading SGT and the driving SGT, is formed to contact at side surface of entire gate region in a vertical direction between the Si pillars 6f, 6g, and 6h.

title: Novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board
abstract: The utility model discloses a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board. The novel material layer structure comprises a cured PI film, an upper semi-cured TFP film coated on thefront surface of the cured PI film, and an upper copper foil layer laminated on the upper semi-cured TFP film. The novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board provided by the utility model has high-frequency characteristics, has the performance of high-speed transmission of high-frequency signals, can adapt to the current high-frequency high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal application, and is especially suitable for novel 5G and 6G scientific and technological products; as an integral structure, the novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board can be used as a circuit board manufacturing material in a subsequent circuit board manufacturing process to manufacture circuit board structures such as a single-layer circuit board, a mul

title: Power amplifier for sub-6G frequency band
assignee: HANGZHOU LINGXIN MICRO ELECTRONICS CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a power amplifier for a sub 6G frequency band, and the power amplifier comprises a complementary power amplifier circuit, of which the bias voltage is provided by a reference voltage circuit; the starting circuit, the positive temperature coefficient reference circuit, the negative temperature coefficient reference circuit and the bias voltage circuit are connected with thepositive temperature coefficient reference circuit and the negative temperature coefficient reference circuit respectively, the first bias current and the second bias current are set according to a certain proportion, and finally reference voltage is generated; the linearity index of the power amplifier is improved and stabilized through a complementary structure and temperature compensation.

title: Radio frequency switch for sub-6G frequency band
assignee: HANGZHOU LINGXIN MICRO ELECTRONICS CO LTD
abstract: With the rapid development of the radio frequency integrated circuit technology, wireless communication products have been widely applied to all walks of life, a radio frequency switch is located at the front end of a wireless receiving system, and the performance of the whole receiving and transmitting system is determined by the performance of the radio frequency switch. The invention provides aradio frequency switch for a sub 6G frequency band, the radio frequency switch adopts a two-stage structure, and the radio frequency switch not only has a wider frequency band range, but also can effectively reduce the parasitic capacitance from a radio frequency antenna to the ground, improves the isolation degree between ports, effectively improves the linearity of a circuit and reduces the insertion loss. An anti-static module adopts a single-tube structure, so that the anti-static capability of the radio frequency switch is improved as much as possible under the condition of reducing paras

title: ELECTRONIC CIRCUIT
abstract: An electronic circuit (4) comprises at least one electrically conductive portion arranged to a substrate (2) and at least one electrical coupling point (5) determined at the at least one electrically conductive portion. The electronic circuit (4) comprises at the at least one electrical coupling point (5) at least one magnetic and electrically conductive coupling element (6a, 6b, 6c, 6d, 6e, 6f, 6g, 6h, 6i, 6j, 6k, 6l) for providing an electrically conductive coupling point (5) with magnetic fastening force.

title: Terahertz antenna suitable for 6G communication frequency band
abstract: The utility model discloses a terahertz antenna suitable for a 6G communication frequency band, and belongs to the field of terahertz antennas. A main body part of the antenna is an insulating dielectric layer, a butterfly-like radiation patch and a circular patch combined with the butterfly-like radiation patch are arranged at the center of the upper surface of the dielectric layer, the butterfly-like radiation patch and the circular patch are fed together, and the circular patch is combined in the radiation patch, so that an ultra-wideband effect is realized; semicircular grooves are symmetrically formed from the left end and the right end of the radiation patch to the farthest end of the central position of the radiation patch, two groups of four rectangular grooves with the same widthare formed from the left end and the right end of the radiation patch to the central position of the radiation patch, and finally a long vertical rectangular groove is formed in the central symmetry po

title: SaaS-based identity recognition and payment method in remote monitoring system
abstract: The invention provides an SaaS (Software as a Service)-based identity recognition and payment method in a remote monitoring system, which comprises the following steps of: acquiring user sign information data, user identity information and user expense payment authorization information at a terminal, performing user identity information recognition and user expense payment authorization verification at a management platform, and paying the expense, wherein the management platform communicates with the terminal through a 5G or 6G wireless network. According to the SaaS-based identity recognition and payment method in the remote monitoring system, the collected user physical sign information is prevented from being confused with another due to human reasons or accidental reasons, and remotemonitoring service interruption due to the fact that service fees are not paid in time is avoided.

title: 6G RIS-assisted physical layer security joint optimization method in NOMA system
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of wireless communication, and discloses a 6G RIS-assisted physical layer security joint optimization method in a NOMA system. The method comprises the steps: in a downlink MISO communication network with one base station BS and two users U1 (a near-end user) and U2 (a far-end user), for the two users are located in a communication dead zone of the basestation, using RIS for auxiliary transmission, enabling the demodulation sequence of a user U1 to be prior to U2, and obtaining demodulation signal-to-noise ratios of the user ends U1 and U2; defining a security rate of the system; constructing a joint optimization equation; and dividing the joint optimization equation into two steps of beam forming and power distribution to be solved. A system comprises a power distribution module; a security rate definition module; a joint optimization equation construction module; and a joint optimization equation solving module. title: NATURAL GRAPHITE, MODIFIED NATURAL GRAPHITE MATERIAL PREPARED THEREFROM, PREPARATION METHOD, AND APPLICATIONS
assignee: BTR NEW MAT GROUP CO LTD [CN]
abstract: Provided are a natural graphite, a modified natural graphite material prepared therefrom, a preparation method, and applications. The natural graphite has the following characteristics: a particle size D50 smaller than 10µm, and D90/D10<2.5; a 002 interplanar spacing ranging from 0.336 to 0.360 nm, I002/I110≤35; a saturated tap density greater than or equal to 0.6g/cc and smaller than 1.3g/cc; and a specific surface area greater than or equal to 1.0m<2>/g and smaller than 10.0m<2>/g. The provided natural graphite has specific particle size, orientation, tap density and surface area and can serve as a raw material for preparing modified natural graphite material, and the prepared modified natural graphite material has high isotropy and thus has better rate performance and cycle performance.

title: Timer services
abstract: A request 6a to apply an update to a given function configured as replicas of the given function on a plurality of nodes 112 in a cluster of nodes providing replicas of functions is received from a client 110 at a first node 112a. The update request comprises an identifier for the given function. The given function identifier comprises information encoded using a probabilistic set such as a bloom filter or quotient filter. The information is associated with each of the nodes in the plurality of nodes on which the given function has been configured. The encoded information is decoded 6b to identify each of the nodes in the plurality of nodes on which respective replicas of the given function have been configured. At least one replica function update message 6c, 6e is transmitted to one or more of the identified nodes to instruct update 6d, 6f of their respective configured replicas of the given function according to the update requested by the client. title: CSI METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CSI PARAMETER CONFIGURATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate after a 4G communication system, such as LTE. A method of operating user equipment (UE) of the present disclosure comprises the processes of: receiving CSI feedback configuration information; generating CSI feedback based on the CSI feedback configuration information; and transmitting the CSI feedback to a BS through an uplink channel.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SHARING FREQUENCY RESOURCES BETWEEN MOBILE COMMUNICATION PROVIDERS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. A method of communicating with a user equipment by using a frequency resource of a second mobile communication provider in a mobile communication system, the method being performed by a first base station of a first mobile communication provider is provided. The method includes requesting to permit the first base station to use a second frequency resource of the second mobile communication provider, transmitting, to the user equipment, setting information, for establishing communication using the second frequency resource of the second mobile communication provider, through a first frequency resource of the first mobile communication provider, and communicating with the user equipment through the second frequency resource, based on the setting information.

title: Mobile terminal

title: ELASTIC WAVE DEVICE
abstract: To provide an elastic wave device in which peeling off of a support member from a piezoelectric substrate can be prevented.SOLUTION: An elastic wave device 1 comprises: a piezoelectric substrate 2 having first and second main surfaces 2a and 2b; an IDT electrode provided on the first main surface 2a of the piezoelectric substrate 2; a support member 6 that has a rectangular frame shape, includes an opening 6a, and includes first and second sides 6c and 6d and a first corner portion 6g in which the first and second sides 6c and 6d are connected to each other, and that is provided on the first main surface 2a of the piezoelectric substrate 2 in such a manner that the support member 6 surround the IDT electrode by the opening 6a; and a cover member provided on the support member 6 in such a manner that the cover member covers the opening 6a. title: CCN-based new node value and content popularity caching method in 6G
abstract: The invention discloses a CCN-based new node value and content popularity caching method in 6G, and the method comprises the steps that: content is cached in a CCN to support various functions, including content distribution and multicast; a node records the corresponding state and interface information in an interest packet request process, and a data packet is skipped back to a user according tothe information; through a content repository CS, a forwarding information base FIB and a pending interest table PIT are kept inside the CCN node; the popularity of the content is estimated through content request counting in the measurement process, so that the utilization rate of the cache space is further improved, and better cache performance is realized in the CCN; a variable is designed tomatch content popularity and a given node value, a proposed caching strategy is evaluated by an NDN-SIM simulator, and then simulation result analysis is carried out. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SHARING FREQUENCY RESOURCES BETWEEN MOBILE COMMUNICATION PROVIDERS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. A method of communicating with a user equipment by using a frequency resource of a second mobile communication provider in a mobile communication system, the method being performed by a first base station of a first mobile communication provider is provided. The method includes requesting to permit the first base station to use a second frequency resource of the second mobile communication provider, transmitting, to the user equipment, setting information, for establishing communication using the second frequency resource of the second mobile communication provider, through a first frequency resource of the first mobile communication provider, and communicating with the user equipment through the second frequency resource, based on the setting information.

title: DISPLAY DEVICE, DISPLAY DEVICE MANUFACTURING METHOD, DISPLAY DEVICE MANUFACTURING APPARATUS
abstract: A display device (1) comprises a shared hole transport layer (6C) shared by a plurality of subpixels (2R, 2G, 2B) between an anode (4) and light emitting layers (8R, 8G, 8B), and an individual hole transport layer (6P) is individually provided for each of the plurality of subpixels between the common hole transport layer and the light emitting layers. The shared hole transport layer is configuredusing a first hole transport layer material. A first individual hole transport layer (6B) included in a first-color sub-pixel (2B) is configured using a second hole transport layer material. Second individual hole transport layers (6R, 6G) included in the second-color subpixels (2R, 2G) is configured using a mixed material of the first hole transport layer material and the second hole transport layer material.

title: Antenna device covering Sub-6G frequency band
abstract: The utility model discloses an antenna structure for Sub6G, comprising a substrate, a PCB board, an antenna module, a feed line and a ground wire, the PCB board and the antenna module are respectivelyarranged on the substrate, the antenna module is connected with the feed port on the PCB board through the feed line, and the ground wire is connected with the feed port on the PCB board through thefeed line. The antenna module is connected with a grounding port on the PCB through the ground wire; the feed-in line comprises a feed-in main line and a feed-in branch, one end of the feed-in branchis connected with the feed-in main line, and the other end of the feed-in branch is a free end. title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR TRANSMITTING AND RECEIVING ASYNCHRONOUS SIGNAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-generation (4G) system. The disclosure relates to an operating method of a terminal in a wireless communication system. The method may include: receiving configuration information about a plurality of signatures for non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) from a base station, the plurality of signatures including first signatures and second signatures; identifying a first signature sequence for the terminal, based on the configuration information; and transmitting data, configured based on the identified first signature sequence, to the base station, wherein the first signature sequence may be configured by repeating the first signatures, and the first signatures may be respectively applied to consecutive symbols of the data.

title: Antenna assembly and electronic equipment
assignee: GUANGDONG OPPO MOBILE TELECOMMUNICATIONS CORP LTD
abstract: The invention relates to an antenna assembly and electronic equipment. The antenna assembly comprises a first antenna and a second antenna; the first antenna comprises a first radiator; the second antenna comprises a second radiator, the second radiator is arranged inside the first radiator, a first projection area on the first radiator is provided with an opening, and the first projection area isa projection area of the second radiator on the first radiator; and the frequency band of the first antenna is a sub 6G frequency band, and the frequency band of the second antenna is a millimeter wave frequency band. The second radiator of the second antenna with the millimeter wave frequency band is arranged inside the first radiator of the first antenna with the sub 6G frequency band, and thesecond radiator is exposed out of the opening in the first radiator, so that the stacking of the millimeter wave antenna and the sub 6G antenna is realized, the integration degree of the antenna assembl

title: Double-frequency common-caliber phased-array antenna device suitable for Sub 6G
abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of antennas, and discloses a double-frequency common-caliber phased-array antenna device suitable for Sub 6G, which comprises a first antenna array plane and a second antenna array plane, and the first antenna array surface comprises a plurality of low-frequency antenna units which are uniformly arranged according to a rectangular grid or a triangular grid; the second antenna array surface comprises a plurality of high-frequency antenna units which are uniformly arranged according to a rectangular grid or a triangular grid; and the first antenna array surface and the second antenna array surface are combined in a staggered arrangement mode or a nested mode. According to the embodiment of the invention, firstly, two frequency bands are realized by using two different antenna units; secondly, an array plane is formed according to respective proper array spacing so as to ensure wide-angle scanning performance of two frequency bands; and finally

title: Cloud memory sharing method and system
assignee: FUJIAN TIANQUAN EDUCATION TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a cloud memory sharing method and system. According to the invention, the method comprises the steps that a cloud server pre-loads preset resources into a cloud memory; the client sends a resource loading request to the cloud server; the cloud server receives the resource loading request sent by the client, and synchronizes the preset resources preloaded in the memory to the client; the client receives the preset resources synchronized by the cloud server and writes the preset resources into a local memory. Based on the transmission speed of 5G and 6G high-speed networks, the speed of loading the cloud resources into the memory is higher than the speed of reading the resources from the disk, the resource data is preloaded through the cloud server, the speed of loading the resources into the memory is increased, and meanwhile, a large number of resources do not need to be loaded locally, so that the resource loading efficiency is improved. Therefore, the installa

title: Lead frame preparation method based on etching process
abstract: The invention relates to preparation of a lead frame of an integrated circuit, in particular to a lead frame preparation method based on an etching process, and aims to solve the technical problem ofoverhigh production cost due to the fact that photoresist can only be used once in the processing process of an existing etching method. The preparation method comprises the following steps: processing a metal sheet or a polymer film material into a mask plate of a lead frame pattern, and coating one surface of the mask plate with viscous polymer materials such as PDMS; tightly attaching the face,provided with the viscous polymer material, of the mask plate to a copper base material to be etched and pressed in vacuum; placing the substrate in a FeCl3 solution with the temperature of 40-100 DEG C and the concentration of 0.1 g/ml-0. 6g/ml for etching; and taking down the etched copper base material from the mask plate to obtain the required lead frame.
